_comment_llvm_triple: aarch64-pc-windows-gnu is the analogue of x86_64-pc-windows-gnu: Clang defines __MINGW32__ so compiler-rt builds, and the object format is COFF/PE which is what UEFI wants. The Microsoft x64 vs SysV split that complicates the x86_64 thunk does not exist on aarch64 — there is exactly one calling convention (AAPCS64) shared by Windows and Linux ABIs, so the thunks just need to slide arg registers.
_comment_no_red_zone: No `-mno-red-zone` here — the red zone is an amd64-only concept (the System V x86_64 ABI reserves 128 bytes below RSP for leaf functions). AArch64 has no equivalent.
_comment_no_soft_float: No soft-float either: NEON / FP is mandatory in ARMv8-A baseline and the firmware leaves it usable. (Unlike x86_64 where we disable SSE because some firmware does not save XMM registers across calls before ExitBootServices.)
